nodes.context: Chinese American criminal organization and street gang founded in San Francisco in 1964.
Gambling establishment in San Francisco's Chinatown where Wah Ching founders worked.
Tong that fought a war with Wah Ching for control of Chinatown's rackets.
Chinese gang involved in the 1977 Golden Dragon Massacre.
Gang that competed with Wah Ching in Los Angeles Chinatown during the 1960s-1970s.
Triad that killed Wah Ching leader Danny Wong in 1991.
Gang founded by former Wah Ching members who defected to Wo Hop To.
Rival Asian gang in Los Angeles in ongoing conflict with Wah Ching.
Gang aligned with Asian Boyz in opposition to Wah Ching.
Hong Kong triad with which Wah Ching has developed strong associations.
Hong Kong triad with which Wah Ching has developed strong associations.
Street gang affiliated with Flip-Side, a purported side of Wah Ching.

edges.evidence_quote: Beginning in 1969, the Wah Ching fought a war with the Suey Sing Tong for control of Chinatown's gambling, loan-sharking and extortion rackets.
edges.context: Wah Ching fought a war with Suey Sing Tong for control of Chinatown rackets.

edges.evidence_quote: Wah Ching first received widespread media attention because of the 1977 Golden Dragon Massacre (GDM) involving another Chinese gang, the Chung Ching Yee, or Joe Boys (JBS).
edges.context: Wah Ching was involved in the Golden Dragon Massacre with the Joe Boys.

edges.evidence_quote: During the 1960s and 1970s in Chinatown, Los Angeles, the contest for gang dominance was between the Joe Boys, Yao Lai, and the Wah Ching.
edges.context: Wah Ching competed with Yao Lai for dominance in Los Angeles Chinatown.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1991, Danny "Ah Pai" Wong, the leader of Wah Ching, was shot and killed by Wo Hop To (WHT) hitmen from Seattle Chinatown.
edges.context: Wo Hop To killed Wah Ching's leader, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: After law enforcement cracked down on the Wo Hop To around 1992, former Wah Ching members who had defected to the Wo Hop To founded the Jackson Street Boys
edges.context: Jackson Street Boys were founded by former Wah Ching members who had defected to Wo Hop To.

edges.evidence_quote: The Wah Ching Gang has an ongoing conflict with another rival Asian gang in Los Angeles, the Asian Boyz (ABZ)
edges.context: Wah Ching has an ongoing conflict with Asian Boyz.

edges.evidence_quote: the Asian Boyz (ABZ), who also aligned themselves with the Vietnamese Boyz (VBZ) in opposition to Wah Ching.
edges.context: Asian Boyz aligned with Vietnamese Boyz against Wah Ching.

edges.evidence_quote: There is law enforcement anecdotal evidence that suggests as a group the gang has developed strong associations with other Asian organized crime groups, such as the 14K and Sun Yee On triads in Hong Kong.
edges.context: Wah Ching has strong associations with 14K triad.

edges.evidence_quote: There is law enforcement anecdotal evidence that suggests as a group the gang has developed strong associations with other Asian organized crime groups, such as the 14K and Sun Yee On triads in Hong Kong.
edges.context: Wah Ching has strong associations with Sun Yee On triad.

edges.evidence_quote: Flip-Side (FS-23) – A purported side of the Wah Ching composed mainly of Filipinos, Flip-Side was believed to be the side of Wah Ching that was affiliated the with Bloods.
edges.context: A side of Wah Ching is affiliated with the Bloods.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 14K, Hop Sing Tong, Suey Sing Tong, Sun Yee On, Tiny Rascal Gang, Bloods
edges.context: Infobox lists Hop Sing Tong as an ally.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 14K, Hop Sing Tong, Suey Sing Tong, Sun Yee On, Tiny Rascal Gang, Bloods
edges.context: Infobox lists Suey Sing Tong as an ally, despite earlier conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 14K, Hop Sing Tong, Suey Sing Tong, Sun Yee On, Tiny Rascal Gang, Bloods
edges.context: Infobox lists Tiny Rascal Gang as an ally.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Asian Boyz, Bamboo Union, Black Dragons, Chung Ching Yee, Wo Hop To
edges.context: Infobox lists Bamboo Union as a rival.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Asian Boyz, Bamboo Union, Black Dragons, Chung Ching Yee, Wo Hop To
edges.context: Infobox lists Black Dragons as a rival.
